title: Ancient Memphis: A Walk Through the White Walls
period: Early Dynastic / Old Kingdom, anchored to Kom el-Fakhry and early Ptah precinct research
summary: A compact living-history walk from the Nile edge through an inhabited Memphis district toward an early Ptah precinct reconstruction.

stops.title: Nile Arrival
stops.summary: The visitor begins beside a Nile branch or canal landing, with boats, reeds, mooring posts, storage jars, and the city edge ahead.
referenceNotes: Nile access is essential to Memphis, but this exact landing and canal-edge composition is provisional.

stops.title: White Walls Threshold
stops.summary: A white-plastered mudbrick edge marks the transition from river infrastructure into the ancient city district.
referenceNotes: The White Walls identity is historically important for Memphis, but the exact early city-wall placement and form remain debated.

stops.title: Kom el-Fakhry Street
stops.summary: A narrow mudbrick residential lane uses clustered houses, plastered courtyards, storage bins, jars, and shade cloth.
referenceNotes: Domestic architecture is inferred from Kom el-Fakhry / Memphite settlement archaeology and comparable period material culture.

stops.title: Craftsmen Area
stops.summary: Pottery, basketry, stone dressing, and low workshop structures connect the district to Memphis craft and temple economy.
referenceNotes: Craft activity is supported by Memphis/Ptah associations and workshop references, but this exact compact placement is reconstructed.

stops.title: Early Ptah Precinct
stops.summary: A walled sacred approach marks the transition from city life into the early Ptah / Hwt-ka-Ptah cult precinct.
referenceNotes: The Temple of Ptah is the selected sacred anchor, but the Early Dynastic / Old Kingdom exterior form is inferred rather than directly preserved.

stops.title: Painted Ptah Shrine
stops.summary: Painted walls, incense, ritual objects, and filtered light show a cinematic inner shrine reconstruction.
referenceNotes: The interior is the emotional climax, but exact iconography and layout must remain speculative until source licensing and expert review.
